title: Who decided Unit 01s color scheme
wikitext:
[]: Who decided Unit-01’s color scheme?             Quote Post by Archer » Wed Mar 24, 2021 11:13 pm Was it Yui? Gendo? Did Yui perhaps consult a 4-year-old Shinji on what he thought the coolest color for a giant robot would be? Hell, who decided Unit-00’s colors? And who decided to CHANGE the color from blue to yellow after the repairs? Somehow I don’t feel like Rei would’ve cared about the color of her giant robot. With Unit-02 (and 08 in the Rebuilds) it’s pretty obvious that the pilots chose their own colors, but for the other units it’s a mystery. “Realistically”, it likely does serve a strategic advantage for the Evas to be brightly colored, so that they can stand out from the environment and be easily distinguishable both for the pilots and for mission control. After all, they’re designed for close combat with giant monsters that may or may not even have eyes and likely sense their targets by detecting AT fields. With that in mind, visual camouflage would be pretty useless . Thus, it’s possible that the colors were simply chosen to be bright and distinctive. Under these assumptions, the black paint on Unit-03 can be rationalized as an undercoat/primer before the final color has been applied, and the shiny metal look that Unit-04 is sometimes depicted with is likely just the raw unpainted steel plate armor. ... Yes, this is a dumb question. But I like coming up with in-universe rationalizations for things that we understand obviously exist just to look cool.

[]: Re: Who decided Unit-01’s color scheme?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Mar 25, 2021 5:25 am Archer wrote: Hell, who decided Unit-00’s colors? And who decided to CHANGE the color from blue to yellow after the repairs? Somehow I don’t feel like Rei would’ve cared about the color of her giant robot.

[]: Re: Who decided Unit-01’s color scheme?             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Thu Mar 25, 2021 4:28 pm Maybe it's egotistical on someone's part? Like Purple is the of royalty and maybe the green is representing Yui's eye color? Maybe Gendo did it or Yui? Perhaps it becoming God is to say it is King?

[]: Re: Who decided Unit-01’s color scheme?             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Mar 29, 2021 11:31 pm You know, I never noticed this, but almost all the Evangelion units use colors of the rules of heraldry. Two 'metals': gold (depicted as yellow) and silver (displayed as white). Four main 'tinctures': red, blue, green, and black. Three rare tinctures: purple, tawny (a kind of orange), and sanguine (a kind of red.) Unit 00 is silver and tawny, later silver and blue. Unit 01 is purple and green. Unit 02 is red and tawny. Unit 03 is blue and black. Unit 04 (based on production art) is silver and black. The MPEs are silver and black. Granted, they do break the rules of heraldry in that you're not supposed to have two tinctures together, but still kind of interesting that they all slot into those boxes. Maybe a coincidence, maybe not! Incidentally, the colors of Mari Gras are purple, green, and gold, with purple representing justice, green representing faith, and gold representing power. Could there be room to interpret the orangey color on Unit 01's knees and thumbs as gold? Who knows!

[]: Re: Who decided Unit-01’s color scheme?             Quote Post by ohno2100 » Tue Mar 30, 2021 12:01 am Archer wrote: Did Yui perhaps consult a 4-year-old Shinji on what he thought the coolest color for a giant robot would be?
